title: Indosat wants to be Asia’s neocloud provider
summary: Indonesia’s second-largest service provider, Indosat Ooredoo Hutchison (IOH), is positioning itself as a regional AI infrastructure and neocloud provider, combining GPU compute, AI-native networks and sovereign AI services to capture the next wave of AI demand.

title: T-Mobile fetes AI RAN innovation with Ericsson
summary: T-Mobile and Ericsson are testing new technology that achieved close to a 10% increase in spectrum efficiency and up to 15% boost in downlink throughput compared to legacy methods.

title: Five enterprises, one lesson: AI runs on the infrastructure you already have
summary: How Eli Lilly, Nasdaq, the NHL and other enterprises are retooling storage, networking and data architecture for agentic AI — without replacing what already works.

title: SpaceX signals fight over Big 3’s satellite D2D joint venture
summary: A lot of questions are swirling around the Big 3's D2D JV – and SpaceX execs signaled their intent to fight.

title: AT&T’s busy building open access networks with Gigapower and Forge Fiber 37
summary: According to an executive with a company that does OSS/BSS, “Open access is expanding like wildfire currently, and it's driven by the Tier 1s, so AT&T, Verizon, T Mobile."

title: Layoffs in face of record revenue - What's going on at Cisco?
summary: Chuck Robbins announces $15.8 billion in revenue and a workforce reduction of 4,000 in the same corporate blog.

title: Equinix unveils multi-cloud sovereignty solution
summary: Fabric Geo Zones ensures that rerouted data remains within defined jurisdictions.

title: Opinion: America’s factory floor runs on CBRS
summary: Private 5G is becoming a foundational technology for U.S. manufacturing competitiveness.

title: Charter’s CEO shares thoughts on the company’s big stock drop
summary: The day before Charter’s Q1 2026 earnings its stock was trading at about $238 per share, and today it’s trading at about $149 per share.

title: Opinion: Nokia’s most important AI move has nothing to do with AI
summary: Nokia’s hiring of former Siemens executive Emma Falck signals a strategic pivot toward industrial automation, operational technology and AI-native infrastructure systems.

title: Nordian authorized to resell Starlink internet to businesses and enterprises
summary: OEMs can now embed Nordian's integrated positioning and connectivity platform directly on the production line, allowing them to bring integrated positioning and connectivity to challenging environments.

title: AT&T, T-Mobile and Verizon to form JV for satellite dead zone coverage
summary: AT&T, T-Mobile and Verizon are pooling their spectrum resources and forming a new joint venture to end dead zones in the U.S., including rural areas.

title: Red Hat CTO says these are 3 big things it’s working on with telcos
summary: Verizon talked up how Red Hat is helping it with network transformation, but that’s just one area where the vendor is working with operators.

title: Verizon CEO Dan Schulman is playing to win
summary: Verizon CEO Dan Schulman has been in the top job for more than six months. What’s he got to show for it?

title: Inter Venezuela Taps Harmonic for PON-Based mobile backhaul for 5G growth
summary: Inter Venezuela, the largest private Internet service provider in Venezuela, is powering its new nationwide XGS-PON-based mobile backhaul service with Harmonic’s cOS virtualized core and portfolio of fiber solutions.

title: Uniti Group CEO says the company is open to M&A ‘in the near term’
summary: Uniti’s CEO said if M&A is a tool for increasing shareholder value, the company is open-minded to it.

title: Nokia taps Siemens exec to run mobile infrastructure biz
summary: Nokia’s new president of Mobile Infrastructure (MI) is Emma Falck, most recently the EVP of Products and Smart Infrastructure Buildings at Siemens.

title: Red Hat exec: Sovereign AI could drive CPU demand for inference
summary: GPUs get all the love, but CPUs are poised to play an increasingly important role in AI’s future, Steve Watt, VP and distinguished engineer in Red Hat’s Office of the CTO, told Fierce at the Red Hat Summit in Atlanta.

title: Mediacom advances DOCSIS 4.0 network upgrades with AOI Quantum Bandwidth
summary: AOI is working with Mediacom to accelerate the upgrade and continued expansion of its fiber and coax network infrastructure.

title: FCC gives conditional OK to EchoStar’s spectrum sales to AT&T, SpaceX
summary: EchoStar won FCC approval for its spectrum transactions with AT&T and SpaceX – and it must establish a $2.4 billion escrow account.

title: Red Hat: AI’s foundation must be open source
summary: Red Hat is making a deliberate play to own the operating layer for enterprise AI,” HyperFrame Research CEO Steven Dickens told Fierce at Red Hat Summit.

title: Lumen builds new long-haul broadband route from Seattle to Minneapolis
summary: The new route originates in Seattle, home to MIcrosoft and AWS, but Lumen is not divulging whether this new route has an anchor tenant.

title: EchoStar added 16K wireless subs in Q1
summary: EchoStar’s Boost Mobile and Gen Mobile brands added about 16,000 customers in the first quarter of 2026, far fewer than the 150,000 it added in the year-ago quarter.

title: What you need to know about neocloud hotshot IREN
summary: IREN is one of several neoclouds scrambling to expand beyond bare metal compute capabilities with software.

title: Layoff tracker — Cisco cuts 4,000 jobs
summary: Tech and telecom companies are facing new pressures to pare down their workforces. Here's our rundown of the most consequential layoffs in 2026.
